title: Native schema model validation pass
description: Validate kubectl-doc's internal Structural model against all native Kubernetes resources and identify required extensions beyond the copied CRD Structural shape.
design: After the first native OpenAPI renderer works, iterate through built-in resources from OpenAPI v3 fixtures or a cluster. Each unsupported/lossy schema case should become a small explicit extension to internal/schema rather than arbitrary OpenAPI support.
acceptance_criteria: A broad native-resource fixture/test pass exists. Unsupported native schema cases are documented with diagnostics or implemented as concrete Structural extensions. The project still rejects arbitrary OpenAPI v3 support outside Kubernetes structural schema rules.
notes:
Native schema pass boundary: do not support OpenAPI composition quantors as renderer structure. Keep only Kubernetes int-or-string detection via x-kubernetes-int-or-string or format int-or-string, and validate native coverage against real /openapi/v3 group fixtures.
Observed a real cluster where discovery advertises core/v1 Pods but /openapi/v3/api/v1 omits Pod schemas. kubectl-doc now resolves resource schemas from component GVK metadata first, then operation GVK metadata, and finally falls back to embedded upstream native OpenAPI v3 for missing built-in schemas in the same group-version.
Live apps/v1 Deployment exposed another native schema case: field schemas can carry description/default plus generated single-ref allOf to the real component schema. Treat that exact allOf shape as a reference wrapper and keep general OpenAPI composition unsupported.
Added renderer-level native fixture sweep: embedded OpenAPI v3 GVK schemas are converted through the kube adapter, rendered as YAML at bounded depth, parsed back as YAML, and the apps/v1 Deployment regression asserts spec expands to selector/template instead of spec: {}.
close_reason: Validated the available embedded Kubernetes OpenAPI v3 fixtures through conversion and YAML rendering, added regression coverage for Deployment spec expansion, and implemented concrete native/schema extensions found during the pass: incomplete native OpenAPI fallback, generated single-ref allOf wrappers, and field-local examples.

title: Cluster resource resolution and OpenAPI v3 schemas
description: Resolve kubectl get-style resource selectors from discovery, fetch the selected OpenAPI v3 group-version document, and render native Kubernetes resource schemas through kubectl-doc's internal Structural model.
design: Start with discovery-backed REST/resource matching, ambiguity diagnostics, and latest-version auto-selection for non-interactive modes. Then fetch /openapi/v3 entries and adapt native schemas into the internal Structural model, extending that model only for concrete native-resource cases found during fixture coverage.
acceptance_criteria: Selectors such as deployments, deployments.apps, and deployments.v1.apps resolve deterministically or report ambiguity. Cluster schema rendering fetches OpenAPI v3 only, never v2. Native resources render through the same YAML renderer as CRDs. Tests cover selector resolution, ambiguity, OpenAPI v3 fetching, and representative built-in resources.
close_reason: Implemented discovery-backed RESTMapper selector resolution, OpenAPI v3 index/document fetching, native schema rendering through kubectl-doc Structural, and tests for selector grammar, ambiguity, OpenAPI v3 fetching, and representative apps/batch built-in fixtures.

title: Cluster discovery resource overview
description: Add the first in-cluster path for kubectl-doc: when no CRD file is passed and no resource is selected, load kubeconfig/discovery data and print a deterministic resource overview. This intentionally stops before cluster OpenAPI schema rendering.
design: Use client-go deferred kubeconfig loading and discovery. Build a small internal overview model from served APIResourceLists, grouping resources by API group and collecting served versions. Keep schema rendering on the existing CRD path.
acceptance_criteria: kubectl doc with no -f and no resource loads cluster discovery and prints groups, resources, and served versions. Output is deterministic and excludes subresources. Resource selectors without -f return a clear not-implemented error for schema rendering. Unit tests cover overview construction, rendering, and CLI branching.
close_reason: Implemented cluster discovery overview: client-go loading, deterministic resource/version overview, CLI no-file branch, and tests.

title: MVP: CRD YAML docs generator
description: Implement the first kubectl-doc milestone: initialize the Go/Cobra project and support CRD-file input rendered as deterministic YAML documentation through an internal Kubernetes Structural schema model copied from the CRD structural shape.
design: Start with CRD file mode only. CRD OpenAPI v3 schemas are converted through Kubernetes' CRD structural helper into kubectl-doc's own Structural model. Renderers depend only on the internal model, so later cluster OpenAPI support can adapt native resources without depending on CRD backend types. Do not implement cluster OpenAPI fetching, TUI, browser, man, or Markdown renderers in this milestone.
acceptance_criteria: kubectl doc -f <crd.yaml> [--version v] -o yaml prints syntactically valid YAML; Cobra flags are wired; renderers consume kubectl-doc's internal Structural model; the CRD backend adapts Kubernetes CRD structural schemas into that model; focused golden tests cover the output.
close_reason: Implemented CRD-file YAML MVP with Cobra CLI, internal Structural schema adapter, and golden tests.

title: Generated GitHub README example and CI checks
description: Add a GitHub Markdown example generated by make gen, document it in README, and add GitHub CI checks for generation drift, Go tests, and golangci-lint.
acceptance_criteria: make gen updates the checked-in GitHub Markdown example/README content; CI runs make gen and fails on dirty generated files; CI runs go test ./... and golangci-lint for formatting/linting.
close_reason: Added generated GitHub Markdown example, make gen/check-generated targets, GitHub CI, pinned golangci-lint v2 checks, and README/CONTRIBUTING docs.

title: Markdown all-version pages and field details
description: Finish the core Markdown renderer behavior: render all served versions in one page, add field detail anchors, and introduce first GitHub/Fern dialect differences beyond frontmatter.
acceptance_criteria: -o markdown* --all-versions renders every served version for CRD files and cluster resources; Markdown output includes field detail anchors; GitHub and Fern output differ in useful dialect-specific wrappers; CLI, renderer, and docs tests cover the behavior.
close_reason: Implemented all-version Markdown pages with field anchors and initial GitHub/Fern renderer differences.

title: Markdown column autodetection fallback
description: Markdown output can fall back to 80 columns even when the user's terminal has a wider width because only stdout is checked for terminal dimensions.
acceptance_criteria: Default Markdown column detection checks stdout, stderr, controlling terminal, and COLUMNS before falling back to 80; tests cover stderr and COLUMNS fallback paths.
close_reason: False alarm: output was piped into glow, so stdout was not a terminal and the 80-column fallback was expected behavior. Use --columns to override when piping.

title: Markdown paragraph wrapping
description: Wrap and reindent generated Markdown prose/YAML description comments to a target column width, defaulting to detected terminal width with a stable fallback, and expose --columns for explicit control.
acceptance_criteria: --columns <n> is wired through Cobra validation; Markdown rendering wraps schema description comments to the selected width while preserving YAML comment indentation and paragraph breaks; tests cover explicit column wrapping and CLI flag behavior.
close_reason: Implemented --columns validation and Markdown paragraph wrapping for YAML description comments, defaulting to detected terminal width with fallback 80, with renderer and CLI tests.

title: Interactive Fern MDX renderer
description: Add a Fern-specific interactive renderer target that emits MDX/custom component integration for foldable schema docs, using the same schema tree and search model as the generic HTML renderer where possible.
acceptance_criteria: Renderer target is designed and wired into CLI output selection; generated MDX can render foldable schema sections in Fern; implementation does not require kubectl-doc generic static HTML JavaScript when Fern components/custom React can provide the interaction; tests cover generated MDX shape.

title: OpenAPI example blobs in schema output
description: Preserve OpenAPI field examples from schema example/examples blobs and use them in generated documentation output when no default is present.
design: Extend the internal schema/documentation model to carry field-local examples from OpenAPI v3 and CRD schemas. YAML rendering should use an example value before enum or placeholder fallback when the example can be represented as valid YAML, and add compact inline metadata like # example string, # example object, or # example array. Rich renderers should expose the same example metadata in hover/focus/details surfaces.
acceptance_criteria: Schema adapters preserve OpenAPI example/examples blobs. YAML output renders example values when no default is present and annotates them with # example <type>. Defaults still take precedence over examples. Tests cover scalar, object, array, named example, and default-over-example cases.
close_reason: Implemented internal example preservation for CRD and native OpenAPI schemas, YAML example rendering with default precedence, named example metadata, and tests for scalar, object, array, named, and default-over-example cases.

title: Path zoom for schema renderers
description: Add -p/--path <json-path> so users can zoom kubectl-doc output to a selected schema field or sub-value after resource/version selection.
design: Resolve the requested JSON Path against the normalized documentation field tree, not raw OpenAPI. Static renderers should render the selected subtree as the root. Interactive renderers should start with the selected field focused while preserving navigation context where practical.
acceptance_criteria: Cobra wires -p/--path. Invalid paths produce a clear error. YAML, Markdown, HTML/browser, man, and TUI renderer paths are defined against the same documentation model. Tests cover at least a Deployment spec/template path and a missing path.

title: TUI renderer and shared search
description: Add interactive terminal documentation mode with split-screen field details, resource/version navigation, folding, and search.
design: Use -o tui and -i for interactive terminal mode. Cursor focus moves by schema field/sub-item JSON path. Left moves to parent, right moves to first child/down, Enter toggles folds. A lower horizontal pane shows details for the focused field. / searches field names and descriptions; // searches only field names; Esc exits search; n/p/up/down navigate highlighted results. q and F10 quit.
acceptance_criteria: TUI starts in group/resource/version navigation when no resource is selected. Selected schemas show foldable YAML plus a details pane. Search highlights matches in strong orange. Keyboard behavior matches the design. No y key binding and no copy command are present.

title: Static HTML and browser output
description: Add static HTML output and browser mode for interactive foldable YAML documentation.
design: -o html writes a static document to stdout. -o browser/-w starts a localhost server on port 0, fetches cluster OpenAPI through the kubectl-doc process, and lives until Ctrl-C. Browser interaction mirrors text/TUI behavior where practical, including fold controls and search.
acceptance_criteria: -o html emits a static interactive HTML document. -o browser starts a localhost server on a random port and prints the URL. Browser mode supports folding and / search over field names and descriptions. No copy buttons or copy commands are added.

title: Markdown documentation renderers
description: Add one-page Markdown output for documentation integration, with GitHub Markdown as the default Markdown dialect and Fern-specific support.
design: Build renderers on the shared documentation model. Support -o markdown as markdown-github, plus explicit markdown-github and markdown-fern dialects. Explore only GitHub and Fern dialect features for now.
acceptance_criteria: -o markdown and -o markdown-github render GitHub-friendly Markdown. -o markdown-fern renders Fern-compatible Markdown. Output is one page/file filtered by selected resource/group/version flags. Tests cover dialect differences.

title: Color YAML flow list punctuation
description: Make inline YAML list punctuation easier to read in colored output, especially overview version lists such as ["v2","v1"].
acceptance_criteria: Colored YAML output styles mapping colons plus [, ], and comma punctuation in flow lists with a light-grey syntax style. Tests cover the ANSI styling without changing plain YAML output.
close_reason: Colored YAML now renders mapping colons and flow-list punctuation with a light-grey syntax style.

title: Render multiple overview versions as YAML lists
description: Change cluster overview output so single-version resources render as a scalar version string, while multi-version resources render as a YAML flow sequence sorted latest-first.
acceptance_criteria: Single version remains 'pods: v1'. Multiple versions render like 'deployments: ["v1","v1beta1"]'. Version order prefers GA over beta over alpha, then higher numeric versions. Tests cover builder and renderer output.
close_reason: Overview versions now render as scalar for one version and latest-first YAML flow lists for multiple versions.

title: Color discovery overview output
description: Apply terminal color support to the YAML resource overview path when stdout supports color and --nocolor is not set.
acceptance_criteria: kubectl doc without -f uses the same color detection as CRD YAML rendering. Overview renderer has tests for plain and colored output.
close_reason: Overview YAML now uses the same terminal color support as schema YAML, with renderer coverage.
